expectedClassifications.notes: Imported via barrel @/shared/hooks -> resolves to shared/hooks path -> matches domUtilityPathPatterns. Functionally this is a page-access guard that calls router.replace(), not truly ambient, but the path pattern wins. Correct by rules, questionable semantically.

Relative import './useSystemsUrlState'. No path match, no name match. Tracing source reveals it wraps nuqs useQueryStates for URL-backed state -- a human would call LIKELY_AMBIENT_HOOK. But name gives no signal. UNKNOWN_HOOK is correct given interpreter's design constraints.

Relative import './useSystemsQueries'. Interpreter sees no path match and says UNKNOWN_HOOK. But tracing the source reveals it wraps 5 TanStack Query service hooks from @/services/hooks/queries/insights. Correct human classification is LIKELY_SERVICE_HOOK. The interpreter WILL get this wrong.
